scope.summary: Auto-restore saved accounts on app start: on launch the core loads each saved account from the local store, decrypts its credential (keychain master key), and re-logs-in + re-listens; the frontend shows a brief loading gate and reaches the chat shell with no QR when at least one account restores. An account whose login fails (e.g. expired cookie) is marked reauth-needed and skipped so the others still come online.
goals: command/ restore_sessions loads saved accounts, decrypts + re-logins/listens each via the shared login_and_listen path, and returns the restored profiles
An account that fails to restore is marked reauth-needed (store mark_reauth_needed) and skipped without disturbing the others
Frontend calls restore_sessions at startup behind a loading gate; the QR screen shows only when zero accounts restore
Credentials are decrypted in the core and never cross IPC
nonGoals: Initial credential acquisition (qr-login) and at-rest encryption (secure-cred-store)
Message history hydration internals (message-cache) — restore only triggers load_history
Proactive token-refresh / keepalive beyond what zca-rust does
A reauth-needed re-scan UI affordance (future UI slice)

acceptance.description: command/ restore_sessions compiles and re-logins saved accounts on startup via login_and_listen; cargo build clean.
A failed restore marks the account reauth-needed and skips it; store mark_reauth_needed is exercised offline (delete_removes_account_row) and the restore loop tolerates per-account failure.

Live: relaunch with a saved account auto-reconnects without QR; the core log shows local store opened saved_accounts>0, restore_sessions restored>=1, and no start_qr_login during restore. Verified by the project owner; the captured non-secret log is asserted to contain the restore-complete marker.
